tasks.subject: [chat-microinteractions-polish] [P1] Upgrade MessageItem entrance to spring physics with user-message scale
tasks.description:
Upgrade the entrance animation in `apps/client/src/layers/features/chat/ui/MessageItem.tsx` from duration-based easing to spring physics, and add a role-gated `scale: 0.97 → 1` for user messages only.

## Current Code (lines 147–150)

```tsx
<motion.div
  initial={isNew ? { opacity: 0, y: 8 } : false}
  animate={{ opacity: 1, y: 0 }}
  transition={{ duration: 0.2, ease: [0, 0, 0.2, 1] }}
```

## Required Change

Replace those three props with:

```tsx
<motion.div
  initial={isNew ? { opacity: 0, y: 8, scale: isUser ? 0.97 : 1 } : false}
  animate={{ opacity: 1, y: 0, scale: 1 }}
  transition={{ type: 'spring', stiffness: 320, damping: 28 }}
```

**Three specific changes:**
1. `initial` gains `scale: isUser ? 0.97 : 1` — role-gated initial scale. Only user messages start slightly compressed. Assistant messages are unchanged (scale: 1 = no compression).
2. `animate` gains `scale: 1` — explicit spring target so motion.dev knows where to settle.
3. `transition` changes from `{ duration: 0.2, ease: [0, 0, 0.2, 1] }` to `{ type: 'spring', stiffness: 320, damping: 28 }`. This spring preset is snappy with no bounce and settles in ~250ms.

**The `isUser` variable** is already computed at line 110: `const isUser = message.role === 'user';`. No new state or props are needed.

**Accessibility**: `<MotionConfig reducedMotion="user">` in `App.tsx` wraps the entire app, so under `prefers-reduced-motion`, the scale and spring transitions collapse to instant — no per-component changes needed.

**Non-goals for this task**: Do NOT change any other props on this `motion.div`, do NOT change the `data-testid`, `data-role`, or `className`. Do NOT touch `AutoHideToolCall`, `ToolCallCard`, or any other sub-component.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] New user message renders with `initial` prop containing `scale: 0.97`
- [ ] New assistant message renders with `initial` prop containing `scale: 1` (no compression)
- [ ] History messages (isNew=false) have `initial={false}` (unchanged behavior)
- [ ] `transition` prop uses `{ type: 'spring', stiffness: 320, damping: 28 }` for isNew messages
- [ ] `isUser` is read from the existing line 110 variable, no new code
- [ ] All existing `MessageItem.test.tsx` tests continue to pass (motion mock renders plain divs, animation props are ignored)
- [ ] No TypeScript errors
tasks.activeForm: Upgrading MessageItem entrance animation to spring physics

tasks.subject: [chat-microinteractions-polish] [P1] Implement layoutId sliding active-session background in SessionItem and SessionSidebar
tasks.description:
Replace the CSS class-toggled `bg-secondary` active background in `SessionItem` with a shared `motion.div` bearing `layoutId="active-session-bg"` that physically slides between rows when the active session changes. Also add the `layout` prop to `SidebarContent` in `SessionSidebar`.

## Files to change

1. `apps/client/src/layers/features/session-list/ui/SessionItem.tsx`
2. `apps/client/src/layers/features/session-list/ui/SessionSidebar.tsx`

---

## SessionItem.tsx Changes

### Step 1: Remove bg-secondary from the outer Wrapper className

The outer `Wrapper` div (lines 79–88) currently has:
```tsx
className={cn(
  'group rounded-lg transition-colors duration-150',
  isActive
    ? 'bg-secondary text-foreground border-primary border-l-2'
    : 'hover:bg-secondary/50 border-l-2 border-transparent'
)}
```

Change to (remove `bg-secondary` from active branch, remove `hover:bg-secondary/50` from inactive branch, keep everything else):
```tsx
className={cn(
  'group relative rounded-lg transition-colors duration-150',
  isActive
    ? 'text-foreground border-primary border-l-2'
    : 'border-l-2 border-transparent'
)}
```

Key additions: `relative` is added to the className (provides the positioning context for the absolute `motion.div` child).

### Step 2: Add the layoutId motion.div as the first child inside Wrapper

Immediately after the opening `<Wrapper ...>` tag and before `<div role="button" ...>`, insert:

```tsx
{isActive && (
  <motion.div
    layoutId="active-session-bg"
    className="absolute inset-0 rounded-lg bg-secondary"
    transition={{ type: 'spring', stiffness: 280, damping: 32 }}
  />
)}
```

This single DOM element will be shared across all `SessionItem` instances via `layoutId`. When `isActive` transfers to a different item, the element animates from the old item's bounding box to the new one using FLIP (no additional DOM nodes per item).

### Step 3: Ensure inner children render above the background

The inner clickable `div` (or `motion.div` after task 1.2 is applied) must have `relative z-10` in its className so it renders above the absolute background. This was already specified in task 1.2's className change. If task 1.2 is applied first, this is already done. If applying in isolation, add `relative z-10` to the clickable surface's className.

The `AnimatePresence` + expand panel section further down in the component is unaffected.

---

## SessionSidebar.tsx Changes

Locate `<SidebarContent data-testid="session-list">` (line 135). The `SidebarContent` component is a Shadcn-derived component. Add the `layout` prop:

```tsx
<SidebarContent layout data-testid="session-list">
```

**Why**: The `layout` prop on an ancestor enables motion.dev to correctly measure the `layoutId` element's position when the list scrolls or re-renders. Without it, the position calculation for the sliding background may be incorrect after scroll.

**If SidebarContent does not forward the `layout` prop**: Check whether the TypeScript compiler accepts the prop. If `SidebarContent`'s type signature does not include `layout`, wrap the content in a `motion.div` instead:
```tsx
<SidebarContent data-testid="session-list">
  <motion.div layout>
    {/* existing children */}
  </motion.div>
</SidebarContent>
```
Prefer the direct `layout` prop approach — check the SidebarContent implementation before falling back.

---

## Behavior Details

- **Spring preset**: `{ type: 'spring', stiffness: 280, damping: 32 }` — smooth and deliberate, appropriate for navigation-level motion
- **Cross-group animation**: The `layoutId` element animates across `<SidebarGroup>` boundaries (Today → Yesterday, etc.) because all groups render within the same `<SidebarContent>` positioned ancestor
- **Hover state**: The `hover:bg-secondary/50` class is removed from inactive items since the sliding background handles active-state visual identity. Hover state is now a lighter bg (if desired, can be re-added as a separate hover class — but per spec, removing it is intentional to reduce visual noise)

---

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `bg-secondary` is removed from the `isActive` className branch of the Wrapper
- [ ] `hover:bg-secondary/50` is removed from the inactive className branch
- [ ] `relative` is added to the Wrapper className
- [ ] `{isActive && <motion.div layoutId="active-session-bg" ... />}` is the first child of Wrapper
- [ ] The `motion.div` has `className="absolute inset-0 rounded-lg bg-secondary"` and `transition={{ type: 'spring', stiffness: 280, damping: 32 }}`
- [ ] `SidebarContent` has the `layout` prop (or equivalent)
- [ ] Clicking between sessions causes the background to visually slide (manual test)
- [ ] Background slides correctly between sessions in different time groups (Today → Yesterday)
- [ ] Existing `SessionItem.test.tsx` tests still pass (note: test for `bg-secondary` on Wrapper className will need updating — see task 1.5)
- [ ] No TypeScript errors

tasks.subject: [chat-microinteractions-polish] [P1] Update contributing/animations.md and contributing/design-system.md with new spring presets
tasks.description:
Update two contributing documentation files to document the spring presets introduced by this feature and update the message entrance animation specification.

## Files to update

1. `contributing/animations.md`
2. `contributing/design-system.md`

---

## contributing/animations.md Changes

### 1. Add a Spring Preset Reference table

Add a new section after the existing "Spring vs Ease Transitions" section. Find the heading `### Spring vs Ease Transitions` and append the following section immediately after its code block:

```markdown
### Chat Microinteraction Spring Presets

DorkOS uses a small set of named spring presets for chat interactions. Prefer these over ad-hoc values for consistency:

| Use case | Preset | Character |
|---|---|---|
| Message entry (new messages) | `{ type: 'spring', stiffness: 320, damping: 28 }` | Snappy, no bounce, settles ~250ms |
| Sidebar active indicator slide | `{ type: 'spring', stiffness: 280, damping: 32 }` | Smooth, deliberate navigation feel |
| Tap feedback (buttons, session rows) | `{ type: 'spring', stiffness: 400, damping: 30 }` | Quick response, already used in ToolCallCard chevron |
| Session crossfade | `{ duration: 0.15, ease: 'easeInOut' }` | Linear opacity — intentional, not spring |

Session crossfade uses duration-based easing rather than spring physics because opacity fades are perceptually linear and a spring would add unnecessary overshoot to a simple visibility transition.
```

### 2. Update the layoutId shared element pattern note

Locate the existing `### LayoutId Selection Indicator` section. After the "Key details" bullet list, add:

```markdown
**Session sidebar usage**: The `SessionItem` component uses `layoutId="active-session-bg"` for the active session background. The `SidebarContent` ancestor carries the `layout` prop to enable correct position measurement during list scroll. The spring preset for this indicator is `{ type: 'spring', stiffness: 280, damping: 32 }` (smooth slide, not the snappier button preset).
```

---

## contributing/design-system.md Changes

Locate the Animation Duration Guidelines table or any section describing message entrance animation. Update:

- **Old**: Any reference to `200ms ease-out` for message entrance
- **New**: `spring stiffness:320 damping:28` (settles in ~250ms, no bounce)

Also add a row or note for session switch transitions:
- Session switch: `150ms opacity crossfade, AnimatePresence mode="wait"`

If the design-system.md has a motion spec table, add:

| Animation | Spec |
|---|---|
| Message entrance (new) | `spring stiffness:320 damping:28` — snappy, no bounce |
| Session switch | `150ms opacity crossfade` — AnimatePresence mode="wait", total 300ms |
| Sidebar active indicator | `spring stiffness:280 damping:32` — smooth slide |
| Session row tap | `spring stiffness:400 damping:30` — quick press feedback |

---

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `contributing/animations.md` has a "Chat Microinteraction Spring Presets" table with all four presets
- [ ] `contributing/animations.md` has a note about session sidebar `layoutId` usage in the LayoutId section
- [ ] `contributing/design-system.md` is updated to reflect spring-based message entrance (not 200ms ease-out)
- [ ] `contributing/design-system.md` includes session switch crossfade spec
- [ ] No broken markdown headings or table formatting
- [ ] Docs render correctly (spot-check in a markdown previewer)
tasks.activeForm: Updating animation and design system documentation
